scores.materiality_reasoning: Occupational exclusion affects labor choices and social standing, though framed through 'virtue'.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of 'vulgar arts' and wage-earning industries.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to a lifelong class identity and career path for free men.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Free status is a legal birth-status in Aristotle's Athens.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes contemporary social and philosophical norms of his time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Aristotle is theorizing what *should* be deemed vulgar based on his philosophy of virtue.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being free does not inherently define one as being excluded from labor.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Involves physical health and growth, though limited to athletic training.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically limited to gymnastic intensity and diet for prepubescent boys.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded specifically to the childhood/pre-puberty life stage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age/biological development is strictly immutable at the time of the rule's application.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Greek training practices and Olympic records.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a general agreement ('it is therefore agreed') among educators.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a minor does not conceptually require 'lighter exercise' by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to music is a symbolic and leisure-based cultural resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Refers to the broad field of music and liberal education.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Education is a long-term resource with lifelong influence on character.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Free status is a structural legal category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the educational standards of current and preceding Greek society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports how people 'actually introduce it' as a form of pastime for free men.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a free man does not inherently include 'music' in its definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Social stigma and 'manliness' are status-based and symbolic resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically tied to the profession of music performance.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Professional status and its associated stigma are generally long-term identities.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation is a durable identity but can be changed through effort.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary social attitudes ('we speak of').
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a prevailing social opinion as a factual norm.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Stigma is not part of the definition of 'musician'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from physical performance (labor) is a tangible role change.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to musical performance within education.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The 'release' is permanent once the individual reaches adulthood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Adulthood is a fixed biological and legal stage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Prescribing norms for the contemporary Greek educational system.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixed between a normative proposal and a description of proper practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an adult does not conceptually imply musical exemption.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to education is a social practice with tangible developmental outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a recurring educational requirement in the youths' life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage of youth/education.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Youth is an immutable age category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the existing goals of Greek paideia.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Largely an argument that children *should* be trained this way.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being young does not define one as a music student.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from menial/professional labor has direct social and economic effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the category of professional competitive performance.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to the lifelong status of a free citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Free status is structural and legal.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Athenian social exclusions.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the social logic ('we count performance... as professional') of the time.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Exclusion from flute-playing is not part of being free.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to public entertainment/competitions is a tangible social resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Refers to the recurring institution of public shows and relaxation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Class-based access to shows is a lifelong social arrangement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (mechanics/laborers) is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary audience stratification in Greek cities.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the factual reality of tailored public festivals.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Public entertainment is not part of the definition of 'mechanic'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to specific musical modes is a symbolic/ceremonial resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Very specific to musical harmonies.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to the duration of the 'comparatively old' life stage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Old age is a chronologically fixed stage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to natural and social observations in contemporary life.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixes natural observation ('powers wane') with normative suggestion.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being old does not define one as a user of 'relaxed harmonies'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Education in a specific mode is a social role with character outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Relates to the broader educational curriculum for youth.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Education affects character for the rest of a student's life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age groups (youth) are fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses common Greek educational agreement ('all agree').
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Notes the agreement of society on the Dorian mode's suitability.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The Dorian mode is not part of the definition of a 'minor'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection of wealth/money from seizure is highly material.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers property rights, a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Property ownership is a structural, lifelong status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status is a legal birth-status/identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lysias describes events in Athens during his own lifetime.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Provides a direct historical report of the decree of the Thirty.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a resident alien does not define one as having seized property.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to a legal defense is a concrete legal protection.
scores.generality_reasoning: Legal standing and defense are foundational to personhood.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Right to a trial is a permanent protection of legal personhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status is structural and legal.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a contemporary instance of legal violation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct observation of a specific execution without trial.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Legal defense is not part of the definition of 'metic'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to a funeral from a specific location is a symbolic/social resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Very specific to burial procedures for executed individuals.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Burial is a permanent final transition, though one-off.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status is a stable legal category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author recounts his own brother's contemporary funeral.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual eyewitness account of the restriction and the 'small hut'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Exclusion from home funerals is not part of the definition of a metic.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to political councils is a concrete legal right with power effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad political exclusion affecting life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Political rights are tied to lifelong citizenship/status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary policy of the Thirty Tyrants.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual report of the movement and meeting restrictions.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Political council access is not part of the definition of 'metic'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to burial is a religious and social entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific but fundamental recurring right in Greek life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Burial is a permanent and final state.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes recent contemporary political atrocities in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports these acts as objective injustices against victims.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Burial rights are not part of the definition of 'citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Marriage affects property, lineage, and physical legal standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis for women's status and inheritance.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Marriage typically initiates a long-term/lifelong status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Combined gender and lineage (citizen daughter) is strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary interference in marital rights.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual list of grievances committed by the regime.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Marriage is not part of the definition of 'daughter'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to a trial is a concrete legal protection against execution.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational legal right within the state.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Permanent protection of legal personhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the recent and current Athenian laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Plain fact about the 'extreme penalty of the law' for violations.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A trial is not conceptually part of the word 'citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Character evidence affects the material outcome of a trial (e.g. survival).
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to court procedure.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the duration of a legal trial/defense.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes 'habitual' practice in contemporary Athenian courts.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Plain report of a standard cultural and legal practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Character evidence is not part of the definition of 'citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from execution is a foundational material/bodily right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad protection of life across the citizen body.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to the permanent protection of life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Recounts a specific contemporary event in Eleusis/Salamis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual reporting of a mass execution by the government.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Life protection is not part of the definition of 'citizen'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Assembly participation is a concrete legal right with material power effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad political right spanning many life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong right for male citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary assembly suspension by the Thirty.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports historical events and the disruption of legal meetings.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Assembly participation is not part of the definition of 'citizen'.
